ayahs.textArabic: وَٱلذَّٰرِيَٰتِ ذَرۡوٗا
ayahs.textTransliteration: Waalththariyati tharwan
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: By those [winds] scattering [dust] dispersing
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: By the winds scattering ˹dust˺,

ayahs.textArabic: فَٱلۡحَٰمِلَٰتِ وِقۡرٗا
ayahs.textTransliteration: Faalhamilati wiqran
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And those [clouds] carrying a load [of water]
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: and ˹the clouds˺ loaded with rain,

ayahs.textArabic: فَٱلۡجَٰرِيَٰتِ يُسۡرٗا
ayahs.textTransliteration: Faaljariyati yusran
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And those [ships] sailing with ease
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: and ˹the ships˺ gliding with ease,

ayahs.textArabic: فَٱلۡمُقَسِّمَٰتِ أَمۡرًا
ayahs.textTransliteration: Faalmuqassimati amran
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And those [angels] apportioning [each] matter,
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: and ˹the angels˺ administering affairs by ˹Allah’s˺ command!

ayahs.textArabic: إِنَّمَا تُوعَدُونَ لَصَادِقٞ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Innama tooAAadoona lasadiqun
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Indeed, what you are promised is true.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Indeed, what you are promised is true.

ayahs.textArabic: وَإِنَّ ٱلدِّينَ لَوَٰقِعٞ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Wainna alddeena lawaqiAAun
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And indeed, the recompense is to occur.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: And the Judgment will certainly come to pass.

ayahs.textArabic: وَٱلسَّمَآءِ ذَاتِ ٱلۡحُبُكِ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Waalssamai thati alhubuki
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: By the heaven containing pathways,
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: ˹And˺ by the heavens in their marvellous design!

ayahs.textArabic: إِنَّكُمۡ لَفِي قَوۡلٖ مُّخۡتَلِفٖ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Innakum lafee qawlin mukhtalifin
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Indeed, you are in differing speech.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Surely you are ˹lost˺ in conflicting views ˹regarding the truth˺.

ayahs.textArabic: يُؤۡفَكُ عَنۡهُ مَنۡ أُفِكَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Yufaku AAanhu man ofika
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Deluded away from the Qur'an is he who is deluded.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Only those ˹destined to be˺ deluded are turned away from it.

ayahs.textArabic: قُتِلَ ٱلۡخَرَّٰصُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Qutila alkharrasoona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Destroyed are the falsifiers
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Condemned are the liars—

ayahs.textArabic: ٱلَّذِينَ هُمۡ فِي غَمۡرَةٖ سَاهُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Allatheena hum fee ghamratin sahoona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Who are within a flood [of confusion] and heedless.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: those who are ˹steeped˺ in ignorance, totally heedless.

ayahs.textArabic: يَسۡـَٔلُونَ أَيَّانَ يَوۡمُ ٱلدِّينِ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Yasaloona ayyana yawmu alddeeni
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: They ask, "When is the Day of Recompense?"
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: They ask ˹mockingly˺, “When is this Day of Judgment?”

ayahs.textArabic: يَوۡمَ هُمۡ عَلَى ٱلنَّارِ يُفۡتَنُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Yawma hum AAala alnnari yuftanoona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: [It is] the Day they will be tormented over the Fire
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: ˹It is˺ the Day they will be tormented over the Fire.

ayahs.textArabic: ذُوقُواْ فِتۡنَتَكُمۡ هَٰذَا ٱلَّذِي كُنتُم بِهِۦ تَسۡتَعۡجِلُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Thooqoo fitnatakum hatha allathee kuntum bihi tastaAAjiloona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: [And will be told], "Taste your torment. This is that for which you were impatient."
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: ˹They will be told,˺ “Taste your torment! This is what you sought to hasten.”

ayahs.textArabic: إِنَّ ٱلۡمُتَّقِينَ فِي جَنَّٰتٖ وَعُيُونٍ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Inna almuttaqeena fee jannatin waAAuyoonin
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Indeed, the righteous will be among gardens and springs,
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Indeed, the righteous will be amid Gardens and springs,

ayahs.textArabic: ءَاخِذِينَ مَآ ءَاتَىٰهُمۡ رَبُّهُمۡۚ إِنَّهُمۡ كَانُواْ قَبۡلَ ذَٰلِكَ مُحۡسِنِينَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Akhitheena ma atahum rabbuhum innahum kanoo qabla thalika muhsineena
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Accepting what their Lord has given them. Indeed, they were before that doers of good.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: ˹joyfully˺ receiving what their Lord will grant them. Before this ˹reward˺ they were truly good-doers ˹in the world˺:

ayahs.textArabic: كَانُواْ قَلِيلٗا مِّنَ ٱلَّيۡلِ مَا يَهۡجَعُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Kanoo qaleelan mina allayli ma yahjaAAoona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: They used to sleep but little of the night,
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: they used to sleep only little in the night,

ayahs.textArabic: وَبِٱلۡأَسۡحَارِ هُمۡ يَسۡتَغۡفِرُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Wabialashari hum yastaghfiroona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And in the hours before dawn they would ask forgiveness,
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: and pray for forgiveness before dawn.

ayahs.textArabic: وَفِيٓ أَمۡوَٰلِهِمۡ حَقّٞ لِّلسَّآئِلِ وَٱلۡمَحۡرُومِ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Wafee amwalihim haqqun lilssaili waalmahroomi
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And from their properties was [given] the right of the [needy] petitioner and the deprived.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: And in their wealth there was a rightful share ˹fulfilled˺ for the beggar and the poor.

ayahs.textArabic: وَفِي ٱلۡأَرۡضِ ءَايَٰتٞ لِّلۡمُوقِنِينَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Wafee alardi ayatun lilmooqineena
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And on the earth are signs for the certain [in faith]
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: There are ˹countless˺ signs on earth for those with sure faith,

ayahs.textArabic: وَفِيٓ أَنفُسِكُمۡۚ أَفَلَا تُبۡصِرُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Wafee anfusikum afala tubsiroona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And in yourselves. Then will you not see?
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: as there are within yourselves. Can you not see?

ayahs.textArabic: وَفِي ٱلسَّمَآءِ رِزۡقُكُمۡ وَمَا تُوعَدُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Wafee alssamai rizqukum wama tooAAadoona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And in the heaven is your provision and whatever you are promised.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: In heaven is your sustenance and whatever you are promised.

ayahs.textArabic: فَوَرَبِّ ٱلسَّمَآءِ وَٱلۡأَرۡضِ إِنَّهُۥ لَحَقّٞ مِّثۡلَ مَآ أَنَّكُمۡ تَنطِقُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Fawarabbi alssamai waalardi innahu lahaqqun mithla ma annakum tantiqoona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Then by the Lord of the heaven and earth, indeed, it is truth - just as [sure as] it is that you are speaking.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Then by the Lord of heaven and earth! ˹All˺ this is certainly as true as ˹the fact that˺ you can speak!

ayahs.textArabic: هَلۡ أَتَىٰكَ حَدِيثُ ضَيۡفِ إِبۡرَٰهِيمَ ٱلۡمُكۡرَمِينَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Hal ataka hadeethu dayfi ibraheema almukrameena
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Has there reached you the story of the honored guests of Abraham? -
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Has the story of Abraham’s honoured guests reached you ˹O Prophet˺?

ayahs.textArabic: إِذۡ دَخَلُواْ عَلَيۡهِ فَقَالُواْ سَلَٰمٗاۖ قَالَ سَلَٰمٞ قَوۡمٞ مُّنكَرُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Ith dakhaloo AAalayhi faqaloo salaman qala salamun qawmun munkaroona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: When they entered upon him and said, "[We greet you with] peace." He answered, "[And upon you] peace, [you are] a people unknown.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: ˹Remember˺ when they entered his presence and greeted ˹him with˺, “Peace!” He replied, “Peace ˹be upon you˺!” ˹Then he said to himself,˺ “˹These are˺ an unfamiliar people.”

ayahs.textArabic: فَرَاغَ إِلَىٰٓ أَهۡلِهِۦ فَجَآءَ بِعِجۡلٖ سَمِينٖ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Faragha ila ahlihi fajaa biAAijlin sameenin
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Then he went to his family and came with a fat [roasted] calf
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Then he slipped off to his family and brought a fat ˹roasted˺ calf,

ayahs.textArabic: فَقَرَّبَهُۥٓ إِلَيۡهِمۡ قَالَ أَلَا تَأۡكُلُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Faqarrabahu ilayhim qala ala takuloona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And placed it near them; he said, "Will you not eat?"
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: and placed it before them, asking, “Will you not eat?”

ayahs.textArabic: فَأَوۡجَسَ مِنۡهُمۡ خِيفَةٗۖ قَالُواْ لَا تَخَفۡۖ وَبَشَّرُوهُ بِغُلَٰمٍ عَلِيمٖ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Faawjasa minhum kheefatan qaloo la takhaf wabashsharoohu bighulamin AAaleemin
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And he felt from them apprehension. They said, "Fear not," and gave him good tidings of a learned boy.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: ˹They did not eat,˺ so he grew fearful of them. They reassured ˹him˺, “Do not be afraid,” and gave him good news of a knowledgeable son.

ayahs.textArabic: فَأَقۡبَلَتِ ٱمۡرَأَتُهُۥ فِي صَرَّةٖ فَصَكَّتۡ وَجۡهَهَا وَقَالَتۡ عَجُوزٌ عَقِيمٞ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Faaqbalati imraatuhu fee sarratin fasakkat wajhaha waqalat AAajoozun AAaqeemun
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And his wife approached with a cry [of alarm] and struck her face and said, "[I am] a barren old woman!"
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Then his wife came forward with a cry, clasping her forehead ˹in astonishment˺, exclaiming, “˹A baby from˺ a barren, old woman!”

ayahs.textArabic: قَالُواْ كَذَٰلِكِ قَالَ رَبُّكِۖ إِنَّهُۥ هُوَ ٱلۡحَكِيمُ ٱلۡعَلِيمُ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Qaloo kathaliki qala rabbuki innahu huwa alhakeemu alAAaleemu
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: They said, "Thus has said your Lord; indeed, He is the Wise, the Knowing."
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: They replied, “Such has your Lord decreed. He is truly the All-Wise, All-Knowing.”

ayahs.textArabic: ۞ قَالَ فَمَا خَطۡبُكُمۡ أَيُّهَا ٱلۡمُرۡسَلُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Qala fama khatbukum ayyuha almursaloona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: [Abraham] said, "Then what is your business [here], O messengers?"
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: ˹Later,˺ Abraham asked, “What is your mission, O messengers?”

ayahs.textArabic: قَالُوٓاْ إِنَّآ أُرۡسِلۡنَآ إِلَىٰ قَوۡمٖ مُّجۡرِمِينَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Qaloo inna orsilna ila qawmin mujrimeena
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: They said, "Indeed, we have been sent to a people of criminals
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: They replied, “We have actually been sent to a wicked people,

ayahs.textArabic: لِنُرۡسِلَ عَلَيۡهِمۡ حِجَارَةٗ مِّن طِينٖ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Linursila AAalayhim hijaratan min teenin
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: To send down upon them stones of clay,
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: to send upon them stones of ˹baked˺ clay,

ayahs.textArabic: مُّسَوَّمَةً عِندَ رَبِّكَ لِلۡمُسۡرِفِينَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Musawwamatan AAinda rabbika lilmusrifeena
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Marked in the presence of your Lord for the transgressors."
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: marked by your Lord for the transgressors.”

ayahs.textArabic: فَأَخۡرَجۡنَا مَن كَانَ فِيهَا مِنَ ٱلۡمُؤۡمِنِينَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Faakhrajna man kana feeha mina almumineena
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: So We brought out whoever was in the cities of the believers.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Then ˹before the torment˺ We evacuated the believers from the city.

ayahs.textArabic: فَمَا وَجَدۡنَا فِيهَا غَيۡرَ بَيۡتٖ مِّنَ ٱلۡمُسۡلِمِينَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Fama wajadna feeha ghayra baytin mina almuslimeena
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And We found not within them other than a [single] house of Muslims.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: But We only found one family that had submitted ˹to Allah˺.

ayahs.textArabic: وَتَرَكۡنَا فِيهَآ ءَايَةٗ لِّلَّذِينَ يَخَافُونَ ٱلۡعَذَابَ ٱلۡأَلِيمَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Watarakna feeha ayatan lillatheena yakhafoona alAAathaba alaleema
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And We left therein a sign for those who fear the painful punishment.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: And We have left a sign there ˹as a lesson˺ for those who fear the painful punishment.

ayahs.textArabic: وَفِي مُوسَىٰٓ إِذۡ أَرۡسَلۡنَٰهُ إِلَىٰ فِرۡعَوۡنَ بِسُلۡطَٰنٖ مُّبِينٖ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Wafee moosa ith arsalnahu ila firAAawna bisultanin mubeenin
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And in Moses [was a sign], when We sent him to Pharaoh with clear authority.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: And in ˹the story of˺ Moses ˹was another lesson,˺ when We sent him to Pharaoh with compelling proof,

ayahs.textArabic: فَتَوَلَّىٰ بِرُكۡنِهِۦ وَقَالَ سَٰحِرٌ أَوۡ مَجۡنُونٞ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Fatawalla biruknihi waqala sahirun aw majnoonun
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: But he turned away with his supporters and said," A magician or a madman."
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: but Pharaoh was carried away by his power, saying ˹of Moses˺, “A magician or a madman!”

ayahs.textArabic: فَأَخَذۡنَٰهُ وَجُنُودَهُۥ فَنَبَذۡنَٰهُمۡ فِي ٱلۡيَمِّ وَهُوَ مُلِيمٞ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Faakhathnahu wajunoodahu fanabathnahum fee alyammi wahuwa muleemun
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: So We took him and his soldiers and cast them into the sea, and he was blameworthy.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: So We seized him and his soldiers, casting them into the sea while he was blameworthy.

ayahs.textArabic: وَفِي عَادٍ إِذۡ أَرۡسَلۡنَا عَلَيۡهِمُ ٱلرِّيحَ ٱلۡعَقِيمَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Wafee AAadin ith arsalna AAalayhimu alrreeha alAAaqeema
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And in 'Aad [was a sign], when We sent against them the barren wind.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: And in ˹the story of˺ ’Âd ˹was another lesson,˺ when We sent against them the devastating wind.

ayahs.textArabic: مَا تَذَرُ مِن شَيۡءٍ أَتَتۡ عَلَيۡهِ إِلَّا جَعَلَتۡهُ كَٱلرَّمِيمِ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Ma tatharu min shayin atat AAalayhi illa jaAAalathu kaalrrameemi
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: It left nothing of what it came upon but that it made it like disintegrated ruins.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: There was nothing it came upon that it did not reduce to ashes.

ayahs.textArabic: وَفِي ثَمُودَ إِذۡ قِيلَ لَهُمۡ تَمَتَّعُواْ حَتَّىٰ حِينٖ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Wafee thamooda ith qeela lahum tamattaAAoo hatta heenin
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And in Thamud, when it was said to them, "Enjoy yourselves for a time."
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: And in ˹the story of˺ Thamûd ˹was another lesson,˺ when they were told, “Enjoy yourselves ˹only˺ for a ˹short˺ while.”

ayahs.textArabic: فَعَتَوۡاْ عَنۡ أَمۡرِ رَبِّهِمۡ فَأَخَذَتۡهُمُ ٱلصَّٰعِقَةُ وَهُمۡ يَنظُرُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: FaAAataw AAan amri rabbihim faakhathathumu alssaAAiqatu wahum yanthuroona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: But they were insolent toward the command of their Lord, so the thunderbolt seized them while they were looking on.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Still they persisted in defying the commands of their Lord, so they were overtaken by a ˹mighty˺ blast while they were looking on.

ayahs.textArabic: فَمَا ٱسۡتَطَٰعُواْ مِن قِيَامٖ وَمَا كَانُواْ مُنتَصِرِينَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Fama istataAAoo min qiyamin wama kanoo muntasireena
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And they were unable to arise, nor could they defend themselves.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Then they were not able to rise up, nor were they helped.

ayahs.textArabic: وَقَوۡمَ نُوحٖ مِّن قَبۡلُۖ إِنَّهُمۡ كَانُواْ قَوۡمٗا فَٰسِقِينَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Waqawma noohin min qablu innahum kanoo qawman fasiqeena
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And [We destroyed] the people of Noah before; indeed, they were a people defiantly disobedient.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: And the people of Noah ˹had also been destroyed˺ earlier. They were truly a rebellious people.

ayahs.textArabic: وَٱلسَّمَآءَ بَنَيۡنَٰهَا بِأَيۡيْدٖ وَإِنَّا لَمُوسِعُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Waalssamaa banaynaha biaydin wainna lamoosiAAoona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And the heaven We constructed with strength, and indeed, We are [its] expander.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: We built the universe with ˹great˺ might, and We are certainly expanding ˹it˺.

ayahs.textArabic: وَٱلۡأَرۡضَ فَرَشۡنَٰهَا فَنِعۡمَ ٱلۡمَٰهِدُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Waalarda farashnaha faniAAma almahidoona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And the earth We have spread out, and excellent is the preparer.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: As for the earth, We spread it out. How superbly did We smooth it out!

ayahs.textArabic: وَمِن كُلِّ شَيۡءٍ خَلَقۡنَا زَوۡجَيۡنِ لَعَلَّكُمۡ تَذَكَّرُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Wamin kulli shayin khalaqna zawjayni laAAallakum tathakkaroona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And of all things We created two mates; perhaps you will remember.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: And We created pairs of all things so perhaps you would be mindful.

ayahs.textArabic: فَفِرُّوٓاْ إِلَى ٱللَّهِۖ إِنِّي لَكُم مِّنۡهُ نَذِيرٞ مُّبِينٞ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Fafirroo ila Allahi innee lakum minhu natheerun mubeenun
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: So flee to Allah. Indeed, I am to you from Him a clear warner.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: So ˹proclaim, O Prophet˺: “Flee to Allah! I am truly sent by Him with a clear warning to you.

ayahs.textArabic: وَلَا تَجۡعَلُواْ مَعَ ٱللَّهِ إِلَٰهًا ءَاخَرَۖ إِنِّي لَكُم مِّنۡهُ نَذِيرٞ مُّبِينٞ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Wala tajAAaloo maAAa Allahi ilahan akhara innee lakum minhu natheerun mubeenun
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And do not make [as equal] with Allah another deity. Indeed, I am to you from Him a clear warner.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: And do not set up another god with Allah. I am truly sent by Him with a clear warning to you.”

ayahs.textArabic: كَذَٰلِكَ مَآ أَتَى ٱلَّذِينَ مِن قَبۡلِهِم مِّن رَّسُولٍ إِلَّا قَالُواْ سَاحِرٌ أَوۡ مَجۡنُونٌ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Kathalika ma ata allatheena min qablihim min rasoolin illa qaloo sahirun aw majnoonun
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Similarly, there came not to those before them any messenger except that they said, "A magician or a madman."
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Similarly, no messenger came to those before them without being told: “A magician or a madman!”

ayahs.textArabic: أَتَوَاصَوۡاْ بِهِۦۚ بَلۡ هُمۡ قَوۡمٞ طَاغُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Atawasaw bihi bal hum qawmun taghoona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Did they suggest it to them? Rather, they [themselves] are a transgressing people.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Have they passed this ˹cliché˺ down to one another? In fact, they have ˹all˺ been a transgressing people.

ayahs.textArabic: فَتَوَلَّ عَنۡهُمۡ فَمَآ أَنتَ بِمَلُومٖ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Fatawalla AAanhum fama anta bimaloomin
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: So leave them, [O Muhammad], for you are not to be blamed.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: So ˹now˺ turn away from them ˹O Prophet˺, for you will not be blamed.

ayahs.textArabic: وَذَكِّرۡ فَإِنَّ ٱلذِّكۡرَىٰ تَنفَعُ ٱلۡمُؤۡمِنِينَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Wathakkir fainna alththikra tanfaAAu almumineena
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And remind, for indeed, the reminder benefits the believers.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: But ˹continue to˺ remind. For certainly reminders benefit the believers.

ayahs.textArabic: وَمَا خَلَقۡتُ ٱلۡجِنَّ وَٱلۡإِنسَ إِلَّا لِيَعۡبُدُونِ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Wama khalaqtu aljinna waalinsa illa liyaAAbudooni
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And I did not create the jinn and mankind except to worship Me.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: I did not create jinn and humans except to worship Me.

ayahs.textArabic: مَآ أُرِيدُ مِنۡهُم مِّن رِّزۡقٖ وَمَآ أُرِيدُ أَن يُطۡعِمُونِ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Ma oreedu minhum min rizqin wama oreedu an yutAAimooni
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: I do not want from them any provision, nor do I want them to feed Me.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: I seek no provision from them, nor do I need them to feed Me.

ayahs.textArabic: إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ هُوَ ٱلرَّزَّاقُ ذُو ٱلۡقُوَّةِ ٱلۡمَتِينُ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Inna Allaha huwa alrrazzaqu thoo alquwwati almateenu
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Indeed, it is Allah who is the [continual] Provider, the firm possessor of strength.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Indeed, Allah ˹alone˺ is the Supreme Provider—Lord of all Power, Ever Mighty.

ayahs.textArabic: فَإِنَّ لِلَّذِينَ ظَلَمُواْ ذَنُوبٗا مِّثۡلَ ذَنُوبِ أَصۡحَٰبِهِمۡ فَلَا يَسۡتَعۡجِلُونِ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Fainna lillatheena thalamoo thanooban mithla thanoobi ashabihim fala yastaAAjiloona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And indeed, for those who have wronged is a portion [of punishment] like the portion of their predecessors, so let them not impatiently urge Me.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: The wrongdoers will certainly have a share ˹of the torment˺ like that of their predecessors. So do not let them ask Me to hasten ˹it˺.

ayahs.textArabic: فَوَيۡلٞ لِّلَّذِينَ كَفَرُواْ مِن يَوۡمِهِمُ ٱلَّذِي يُوعَدُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Fawaylun lillatheena kafaroo min yawmihimu allathee yooAAadoona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And woe to those who have disbelieved from their Day which they are promised.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Woe then to the disbelievers when they face their Day which they are warned of!
